name: Agent Playbook
description_html:
Welcome to your project's Agent Playbook.

This is a shared doc where your team captures how you run AI agents on this project — conventions, approval policy, prompts that work, and things that have burned you. Update it as you learn. The clearer this is, the better every future run goes.

🛠 Setup snapshot

Field

Details

Agent in use

Claude Code / Codex (pick one)

Runner host(s)

e.g. dev-mac-01, ci-runner

Default branch

main

Approval policy

manual / auto-allow safe / strict

💡 Keep this small. Detail belongs in the wiki or the docs/ folder.

🧭 What goes in a good work item

  • Title: one specific change in one line.

  • Description: file paths, context, the constraint to satisfy.

  • Acceptance: how the agent (and a reviewer) will know it's done.

Examples:

  • ❌ "Improve auth" — too vague.

  • ✅ "Reject login when email contains uppercase before the normalize step in auth/login.ts. Add a unit test."

🛡 Approval conventions

  • Approve once by default; only approve for session when both the action kind and the run scope are small.

  • Decline if the agent wants to touch files outside the work item's scope.

  • When something goes wrong, add a "Don't do this" entry below so future runs avoid it.

🚫 Don't do this (project quirks)

(Add entries here as you discover them.)

🧪 Prompt patterns that work

(Capture reusable prompt snippets from your wins.)

name: Run Notes
description_html:
This is your private Run Notes scratchpad. Use it to track:

  • Prompts that produced great results — paste them here so you can reuse them.

  • Agent quirks you've noticed (e.g. "always re-runs pnpm install even when not needed").

  • Follow-up tasks the agent uncovered but didn't complete.

It's visible only to you (and collaborators you explicitly share with).

✍ Open items

  • Capture the prompt I used for the auth refactor

  • Try a smaller scope on the next migration

  • Document the "approval fatigue" pattern for the team

  • File a wishlist work item: bulk-approve from the TUI

💡 Promote any of these into actual work items when ready.

🧱 Snippets I want to reuse

Paste prompts, commands, and patterns here.

# Prompt header I keep reusing
You're working in <FILE>. Constraints: <CONSTRAINTS>. Acceptance: <CRITERIA>. Don't touch <FORBIDDEN_PATHS>.
